title: Big Tech Earnings Live: Alphabet Results Top Wall Street Expectations; Tesla Profit Misses Estimates as AI Spending Soars
excerpt: Alphabet and Tesla headlined a busy afternoon of Big Tech earnings reports that will help determine where the AI trade is headed next.
content_html:
Kara Greenberg, Stephen Wisnefski

Wed, July 22, 2026 at 10:51 AM CDT 11 min read

Two of the world's most valuable companies just reported earnings, kicking off what's expected to be another strong earnings season for America's tech giants.

Tesla (TSLA) and Google-parent Alphabet (GOOGL) reported shortly after the closing bell, the first of the Magnificent 7 to release their second-quarter results. Dow component International Business Machines (IBM), enterprise software maker ServiceNow (NOW) and chipmaker Texas Instruments (TXN) also reported this afternoon.

The results could inject fresh enthusiasm into the sector after a pullback in recent weeks, or deepen its slide amid worries about AI stock valuations.

Alphabet shares were down 3% in recent extended trading despite results that topped Wall Street expectations on the top and bottom lines. Tesla stock fell 4% as the EV maker's profit came in well short of analysts' estimates.

Follow along below for all the details from the earnings reports and the market reaction.

JULY 22, 2026 AT 09:20 PM GMT

ServiceNow Raises Its Outlook as Earnings Top Estimates

Shares of ServiceNow climbed 6% in extended trading after the enterprise platform raised its outlook and reported quarterly earnings that topped analysts' estimates.

ServiceNow said it now expects full-year subscription revenue of $15.76 billion to $15.78 billion, up from $15.74 billion to $15.78 billion previously.

The company posted adjusted earnings per share of 90 cents on revenue that jumped 22.5% year-over-year to $3.99 billion for the second quarter, above analysts' projections compiled by Visible Alpha, as subscriptions surged.

CEO Bill McDermott called the results "exceptional" and said they "solidify our position as the fastest-growing major enterprise software and cybersecurity company."

-Kara Greenberg

JULY 22, 2026 AT 09:20 PM GMT

YouTube Ad Sales Boosted by World Cup

Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai says that "people turn to YouTube for major world events." The World Cup is one, and it evidently helped the tech giant sell some ads.

Alphabet reported second-quarter Google Services revenue of $94.5 billion, aided by 13% year-over-year growth in YouTube ads.

Pichai, who also is Google's chief executive, said that YouTube registered "over 1.7 billion unique viewers watching World Cup-related videos during the FIFA World Cup 2026."

(It should be noted that Alphabet's second quarter ended June 30, while the World Cup ran from June 11 until July 19, meaning some of the ad sales will be counted in the company's Q3 results.)

-Aaron Rennie

JULY 22, 2026 AT 08:59 PM GMT

content_html_zh:
卡拉·格林伯格、斯蒂芬·维斯奈夫斯基

2026 年 7 月 22 日星期三上午 10:51 CDT 阅读时间 11 分钟

全球市值最高的两家公司刚刚公布了财报，拉开了美国科技巨头又一个强劲财报季的序幕。

特斯拉 (TSLA) 和谷歌母公司 Alphabet (GOOGL) 在收盘后不久发布了财报，这是 Magnificent 7 中第一个发布第二季度业绩的公司。 道指成分股国际商业机器公司 (IBM)、企业软件制造商 ServiceNow (NOW) 和芯片制造商德州仪器 (TXN) 今天下午也发布了报告。

这一结果可能会给该行业在最近几周的回调后注入新的热情，或者在对人工智能股票估值的担忧中加剧其下滑。

尽管 Alphabet 的营收和利润都超出了华尔街的预期，但其股价在最近的延长交易中下跌了 3%。 由于电动汽车制造商特斯拉的利润远低于分析师的预期，该公司股价下跌 4%。

请跟随以下内容了解收益报告和市场反应的所有详细信息。

格林威治标准时间 2026 年 7 月 22 日晚上 9:20

ServiceNow 上调盈利预期

在企业平台上调前景并报告季度收益超出分析师预期后，ServiceNow 股价在盘后交易中上涨 6%。

ServiceNow表示，目前预计全年订阅收入为157.6亿美元至157.8亿美元，高于此前的157.4亿美元至157.8亿美元。

随着订阅量激增，该公司第二季度调整后每股收益为 90 美分，收入同比增长 22.5% 至 39.9 亿美元，高于 Visible Alpha 编制的分析师预测。

首席执行官比尔·麦克德莫特称这一结果“非常出色”，并表示它们“巩固了我们作为增长最快的大型企业软件和网络安全公司的地位”。

-卡拉·格林伯格

格林威治标准时间 2026 年 7 月 22 日晚上 9:20

世界杯带动 YouTube 广告销量

Alphabet 首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊 (Sundar Pichai) 表示，“人们会通过 YouTube 观看世界重大事件。” 世界杯就是其中之一，它显然帮助这家科技巨头销售了一些广告。

Alphabet 公布第二季度谷歌服务收入为 945 亿美元，得益于 YouTube 广告同比增长 13%。

身兼谷歌首席执行官的皮查伊表示，YouTube“有超过 17 亿独立观众在 2026 年 FIFA 世界杯期间观看世界杯相关视频”。

（值得注意的是，Alphabet 的第二季度于 6 月 30 日结束，而世界杯从 6 月 11 日持续到 7 月 19 日，这意味着部分广告销售额将计入该公司第三季度的业绩。）

-亚伦·雷尼

格林威治标准时间 2026 年 7 月 22 日晚上 8:59
